source.reference: Boiling point of ethanol at 1 atm using provided Antoine coefficients.

problem.statement: Pure ethanol is at equilibrium with its vapor at 1 atm (101.325 kPa). Using the Antoine constants provided in the problem statement, estimate the normal boiling point of ethanol.
problem.task: Calculate the boiling point of ethanol in K.

antoine_ethanol.description: Antoine coefficients for ethanol, log10(P/mmHg) = A - B/(C + T/°C)

must_include: Convert 1 atm to 760 mmHg or equivalent consistent units
Solve the Antoine equation for temperature
must_not_include: Claim external property lookup is required
Assume the boiling point without calculation

key_assumptions: Pure-component vapor pressure is represented adequately by the provided Antoine constants
common_mistakes: Using K directly in an Antoine equation parameterized for °C
Requesting a lookup tool despite all required data being present

reasoning_checkpoints.checkpoint: Recognizes that the provided data is sufficient and no external lookup is required
Uses the Antoine equation with the provided coefficients
Performs the pressure unit conversion consistently
Reports the final answer in K
